title: cli.py: slide list and layouts commands
description: Wire `pypptx slide list <file_or_dir>` and `pypptx slide layouts <file_or_dir>` into the `slide` subgroup. Call `ops.slides.list_slides()` and `ops.slides.list_layouts()` respectively. Format output via `output_result()`.
acceptance_criteria: `pypptx slide list deck.pptx` outputs `{"slides": [{"index": 1, "file": "slide1.xml", "hidden": false}, ...]}` in presentation order.
`pypptx slide list deck.pptx --plain` outputs one slide per line, appending `[hidden]` when applicable.
`pypptx slide layouts deck.pptx --plain` outputs one layout per line.
Both commands accept a directory as input.
Errors go to stderr; exit code is non-zero on failure.

handoff_summary.completed: Implemented `slide list` command: calls `ops.slides.list_slides()`, outputs `{"slides": [...]}` JSON or one-per-line plain text with `[hidden]` suffix. Implemented `slide layouts` command: calls `ops.slides.list_layouts()`, outputs `{"layouts": [...]}` JSON or one-per-line plain text. Both accept file or directory, error to stderr, non-zero exit on failure. Syntax-checked with `py_compile`.
handoff_summary.risks: No known risks. Both ops functions were already implemented in B-9ec19a2c and the CLI wiring is straightforward.
handoff_summary.next_action: Hand off to tester agent to verify acceptance criteria against a real .pptx file.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only `pypptx/cli.py` was modified, which is this bead's exclusive scope.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Plain-text format for `slide list` uses `"filename [hidden]"` pattern consistent with the acceptance criteria. Plain-text format for `slide layouts` is bare filename per line. Lambda inside `output_result` joins all items so the helper's single `"\n".join` call produces the full multi-line block before the trailing newline added by `output_result`.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: No tests written (tester responsibility). Commands should be exercised with a real .pptx and an unpacked directory, including a file with a hidden slide.

conflict_risks: None — only `pypptx/cli.py` was modified, which is this bead's exclusive scope.

execution_history.summary: Added `slide list` and `slide layouts` commands to the `slide` subgroup in `pypptx/cli.py`. Both commands accept a file or directory path, call the corresponding ops functions, handle errors to stderr with non-zero exit, and support `--plain` output (one item per line, with `[hidden]` appended for hidden slides).

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
